Order To Show Cause In Re Contempt (John Giddings)
Off Calendar Dec-06-2013 Continued To Apr-11-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert E. White, Esq. of the Law Office of Robert E. White, Tel. 415.788.6151, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The party stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge. The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested, re Order To Show Cause In Re Contempt (John Giddings). Judgment creditor Glendonbrook Wines moved for civil contempt after judgment debtor John Gidding failed to respond to post-judgment discovery relating to enforcement of the judgment obtained by Glendonbrook against Gidding. On August 23, 2013, this Court granted Glendonbrook's motion to compel responses to the post-judgment discovery. On October 10, 2013, the Court continued the hearing date to allow Glendonbrook to personally serve Gidding. On October 31, 2013, this Court issued an Order to Show Cause in Re Contempt, in which the Court indicated that Gidding's appeared to be contempt of the Court's October 31, 2013 Order, and ordering Gidding to appear on December 6, 2013 to show cause as to why he should not be held in contempt. In connection with its original motion to hold judgment debtor in contempt, Glendonbrook served Gidding at the address provided by him to the Court of Appeals, and also by email to the email address used by Gidding to object to Glendonbrook's notice of levy against a bank account. However, Gidding now appears to moved permanently to Luxembourg. Glendonbrook has provided evidence that it would be costly and time-consuming to effect personal service in Luxembourg, in part because all papers must be translated into a foreign language even though Gidding is a US citizen who speaks fluent English. In addition, Gidding has obviously received notice of the contempt motion because he has referenced it in papers filed objecting to the notice of levy. However, Glendonbrook requests the Court continue the date for the show cause hearing for 120 days to allow Glendonbrook to attempt personal service. The Court grants this request and resets the order to show cause hearing for April 11, 2014 at 9:00 a.m. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Geoffrey Howard; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings not reported.

Robert E. White, Esq., appearing for Judgment Creditor and petitioner Glendonbrook Wine PTY LTD.
Dorian L. Jackson, Esq., appearing for Midshore Marketing, Inc.

Judgment Creditor Glendonbrook Wine PTY LTD.'s Petition to determine Third Party Claim and Motion for Order to Determining Claim of Exemption are heard this day.
Midshore Inc.'s Claim of Exemption is withdrawn by counsel for Midshore.
The following exhibits were marked in order of the court as follows:
Court Exhibit 1. Group Exhibit. Copies Bank of America Checks/Midshore Marketing Inc. to John Gidding.
Court Exhibit 2. Group Exhibit. Copies Bank of America Checks/Midshore Marketing Inc. to San Francisco Superior Court.
Court Exhibit 3. Group Exhibit. Pulltran Report Bank of America.

Declaration of Christopher Goff in re Third Party Claim/Claim of Exemption of Midshore Marketing Inc.
